kill_fact: The catalyst has already FIRED, favorably and irreversibly, so the asymmetric special situation no longer exists. The FDA approved Viridian's lead drug Lumvoa (veligrotug-vvze) for thyroid eye disease on/around June 26, 2026 - independently confirmed by the company's SEC 8-K (EDGAR CIK 0001590750, exhibit 99.1, filed June 25/26 2026), the matching BusinessWire press release dated 2026-06-25, and Viridian IR. This was the single binary that defined the setup, with a PDUFA target action date of June 30, 2026 (confirmed in the BLA-acceptance release). With the verdict in, the cash floor that defined the downside ONLY backstopped the rejection scenario, so it is now moot, and the stock has re-rated from the $16.35 setup to ~$20. The remaining gap to the $35 'win' target is no longer a pre-event spread you are paid to hold a binary through - it is ordinary commercial-execution upside vs an entrenched incumbent (Amgen's Tepezza), carrying ordinary risk, not defined-risk event asymmetry. A fired catalyst with no live, dated, binding forcing function left is exactly the disqualifier this board screens out. (Note: this supersedes the prior 2026-06-19 skeptic pass, which scored conviction_cap 65 while the FDA decision was still pending and live; that pass is now obsolete.)
corrections: Load-bearing facts in the catalyst file all verify: drug brand Lumvoa, generic veligrotug-vvze, approval ~June 26 2026 ahead of the June 30 PDUFA, broad active+chronic label, $762M cash as of Mar 31 2026, ~$20 current price. The file already classifies this correctly as FIRED / verdict C / conviction 2, so no factual fix is needed. One residual nuance carried from the prior verification pass: the ~$8 cash-per-share floor was already soft even pre-approval because Viridian raised ~$350M of dilutive securities in May 2026 ($250M 1.75% convertible notes due 2032 + ~$100M equity/Series B preferred, repaying a ~$50.4M Hercules secured loan), lifting the share/convert count above the ~110M used. That floor is now moot regardless since approval removed the only scenario it protected.
evidence: SEC Form 8-K, Viridian Therapeutics (EDGAR CIK 0001590750), exhibit 99.1, filed June 25/26 2026 - announces U.S. FDA approval and immediate launch of Lumvoa (veligrotug-vvze) for thyroid eye disease (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001590750/000119312526286273/d164211dex991.htm)

BusinessWire, 2026-06-25: 'Viridian Therapeutics Announces U.S. FDA Approval and Launch of Lumvoa (veligrotug-vvze) for the Treatment of Thyroid Eye Disease' - confirms approval, class-first broad active+chronic label, and immediate commercial launch

Viridian IR BLA-acceptance release (2025): FDA accepted the veligrotug BLA under Priority Review with a PDUFA target action date of June 30, 2026 (https://investors.viridiantherapeutics.com/news/news-details/2025/Viridian-Therapeutics-Announces-BLA-Acceptance-and-Priority-Review-for-Veligrotug-for-the-Treatment-of-Thyroid-Eye-Disease/default.aspx)

Viridian Q1 2026 results, BusinessWire 2026-05-05: $762M cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments as of March 31, 2026 - the balance sheet behind the ~$7-8/share cash floor that backstopped only the now-impossible rejection case
